question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper reports HIV sequences from patient samples as it mentions sequencing the pol gene from proviral DNA among viremic and suppressed patients to identify drug resistance mutations.
sentences: Drug resistance mutations archived in resting memory CD4+ cells may persist despite suppression of HIV RNA to <50 copies/ml. We sequenced pol gene from proviral DNA among viremic and suppressed patients to identify drug resistance mutations; HIV-1 pol genotyping by bulk sequencing was performed on 38 DNA and RNA from viremic patients and DNA only among 82 suppressed patients at baseline.

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper does not mention any in vitro drug susceptibility testing. It focuses on sequencing and identifying drug resistance mutations from patient samples.
sentences: Drug resistance mutations archived in resting memory CD4+ cells may persist despite suppression of HIV RNA to <50 copies/ml. We sequenced pol gene from proviral DNA among viremic and suppressed patients to identify drug resistance mutations; HIV-1 pol genotyping by bulk sequencing was performed on 38 DNA and RNA from viremic patients and DNA only among 82 suppressed patients at baseline.

question: Does the paper report GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates? If "Yes", what were the GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates?
explain: The paper explicitly states that the HIV-1 PR and RT sequences recovered from PBMC and viral RNA have been submitted to GenBank with the accession numbers KJ69464 to KJ769641.
sentences: HIV-1 PR and RT sequences recovered from PBMC and viral RNA have been submitted to GenBank with the accession numbers KJ69464 to KJ769641.

question: Which HIV species were studied in the paper?
explain: The paper specifically mentions HIV-1 in multiple sections, including the methods and results.
sentences: The Peninsula AIDS Research Cohort study enrolled and followed over 2 years 120 HIV infected patients from San Mateo and San Francisco Counties. HIV-1 pol genotyping by bulk sequencing was performed on 38 DNA and RNA from viremic patients and DNA only among 82 suppressed patients at baseline; HIV-1 PR and RT sequences recovered from PBMC and viral RNA have been submitted to GenBank with the accession numbers KJ69464 to KJ769641.

question: Which HIV genes were reported to have been sequenced?
answer: A. Protease, PR; B. Reverse transcriptase, RT
explain: The paper mentions sequencing a 1,300-bp fragment encompassing the protease (PR) and the first 300 amino acids of the reverse transcriptase (RT) gene.
sentences: A 1,300-bp fragment encompassing the protease (PR) and the first 300 amino acids of the reverse transcriptase (RT) gene was generated from DNA and cDNA for PCR amplification, followed by a nested PCR with 1st round PCR primers; MAW26 (5´-TTGGAAATGTGGAAAGGAAGGAC-3´) and RT21 (5´-CTGTATTTCTGCTAT TAAGTCTTTTGATGGG-3´) and 2nd round with PRO1 (5´-CAGAGCCAACAGCCC CACCA-3´) and RT20 (5´-CTGCCAGTTCTAGCTCTG CTTC-3´), with Platinum Taq DNA Polymerase (Invitrogen), 50pmoles of each primer, 2mM of Magnesium Chloride and 0.5mM of dNTPs.

question: From which countries were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The paper mentions that the study population was recruited from the Peninsula AIDS Research Consortium clinic at the San Mateo County AIDS Program and the San Francisco Public Health Department clinic, both located in the United States.
sentences: One hundred and twenty HIV infected individuals were recruited from the Peninsula AIDS Research Consortium clinic at the San Mateo County AIDS Program and the San Francisco Public Health Department clinic, and enrolled in a longitudinal study of drug resistance.

question: From what years were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The paper does not provide specific years during which the samples were obtained.

question: What method was used for sequencing?
explain: The paper describes the sequencing process using the Big Dye Terminator kit and capillary electrophoresis on a 3130xl Genetic Analyzer, which are components of Sanger sequencing.
sentences: After quantification, 30ng were sequenced using the Big Dye Terminator kit (Life Technologies, Foster City, CA, USA) and a set of 4 bidirectional primers by capillary electrophoresis on a 3130xl Genetic Analyzer (Life Technologies).

question: Were samples cloned prior to sequencing?
explain: The paper does not mention any cloning of samples prior to sequencing. It describes direct sequencing of PCR products.
sentences: A 1,300-bp fragment encompassing the protease (PR) and the first 300 amino acids of the reverse transcriptase (RT) gene was generated from DNA and cDNA for PCR amplification, followed by a nested PCR with 1st round PCR primers; After quantification, 30ng were sequenced using the Big Dye Terminator kit (Life Technologies, Foster City, CA, USA) and a set of 4 bidirectional primers by capillary electrophoresis on a 3130xl Genetic Analyzer (Life Technologies).

question: Did samples undergo single genome sequencing?
explain: The paper does not mention single genome sequencing. It describes bulk sequencing of PCR products.
sentences: HIV-1 pol genotyping by bulk sequencing was performed on 38 DNA and RNA from viremic patients and DNA only among 82 suppressed patients at baseline.

question: What clinical specimens were sequenced?
explain: The paper mentions that plasma and PBMC were used for sequencing. Plasma was used for RNA extraction, and PBMC were used for DNA extraction.
sentences: Plasma was removed after centrifugation and PBMC were separated from whole blood by density gradient centrifugation and stored at -70°C in 90% human serum, 10% Dimethyl Sulfoxyde (DMSO); Virus was concentrated from 1 ml of plasma by centrifugation for 20 min at 20,000g to pellet the virus, 800μl of supernatant was discarded and RNA extracted from the 200μl residua with QIAamp viral RNA Mini kit (QIAGEN Inc., Hilden, Germany) as per the manufacturer's protocol; DNA was extracted from pelleted PBMC using QIAamp DNA Blood Mini kit (QIAGEN Inc.).

question: Were any sequences obtained from individuals with virological failure on a treatment regimen?
explain: The paper mentions that sequences were obtained from patients who virologically failed during follow-up.
sentences: Only the vRNA was sequenced from patients who virologically failed during follow-up (n = 11).

question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from individuals who had previously received ARV drugs?
explain: The paper mentions that 81% of the subjects were on antiretroviral therapy and had been treated for a median time of 7 years.

question: Which drug classes were received by individuals in the study before sample sequencing?
answer: A. NRTI; B. NNRTI; C. PI
explain: The paper mentions that the cumulative ART history included exposure to NRTIs, NNRTIs, and PIs.
sentences: Cumulative ART history included exposure to TDF (85%), AZT and/or d4T (57%) and 57% and 66% had received an NNRTI- or PI- based regimen respectively.
